lessons.title: Numbers 11-15
lessons.topic: Counting colorful balloons at a party
lessons.context: Counting colorful balloons at a party.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What colors can balloons be at a party?
questions.hint: Think about the bright, happy colors you see at birthday parties!

questions.question_text: Where do we see lots of balloons?
questions.hint: Where do we celebrate with cake, friends, and fun decorations?

questions.question_text: What happens when we count balloons?
questions.hint: When we count things, we use special words that help us know how many!

items.definition: A number after ten.
The number 12.
A number after twelve.
A number after thirteen.
A number after fourteen.
The number that comes after ten
The number that comes after eleven
The number that comes after twelve
The number that comes after thirteen
The number that comes after fourteen

steps.title: Emma's Birthday Party
steps.text: Today is Emma's birthday party! There are eleven red balloons and twelve blue balloons. Emma counts thirteen yellow balloons too. Her friend Max brings fourteen green balloons. Now there are fifteen pink balloons on the wall. What a colorful party!
steps.scene_description: children at a colorful birthday party with many balloons in different colors

questions.question: How many red balloons are there?
Who brings the green balloons?
Are there fifteen pink balloons?

steps.rule_pattern: How many ___ are there? There are ___ ___ ___.
steps.explanation: We use 'How many' to ask about the number of things, and we answer with 'There are' plus the number, color, and object.
examples: How many balloons are there? There are eleven red balloons.
How many balloons are there? There are thirteen yellow balloons.
How many balloons are there? There are fifteen pink balloons.

items.correctSentence: How many green balloons are there?

items.sentence: There are fifteen pink balloons at party.

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Look at the party! How many red balloons are there? Let's count together. There are eleven red balloons. Now look at the blue balloons. There are twelve blue balloons. Can you see the yellow balloons? There are thirteen yellow balloons at the party!

questions.question: How many red balloons are there?
How many blue balloons are there?
What color are the thirteen balloons?

steps.prompt: Look at the pictures and practice speaking about the balloons!

prompts.prompt: Look at the picture. Say: There are eleven red balloons.
Look at the picture. Say: There are thirteen yellow balloons.
Look at the picture. Ask your friend: How many blue balloons are there?
prompts.expected_text: How many blue balloons are there?

questions.question_text: What number comes after twelve?
How many balloons ___ there?
Read: 'There are fifteen pink balloons at the party.' How many pink balloons are there?
True or False: We say 'There are' with many balloons.
Which sentence is correct?

lessons.title: Numbers 16-20
lessons.topic: Counting books on a library shelf
lessons.context: Counting books on a library shelf.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where do we find books?
questions.hint: Think about where we keep books at home or in a library!

questions.question_text: How many books can you see if you count: one book, two books, three books?
questions.hint: Count with me: one, two, three!

questions.question_text: What can we do with books?
questions.hint: We open books and look at the words and pictures!

items.definition: The number after fifteen.
The number after sixteen.
The number after seventeen.
The number after eighteen.
The number 20.

items.sentence: I can count ___ books on the shelf!
There are ___ apples in the basket!
I see ___ birds in the tree!
We have ___ pencils in our box!

steps.title: Books at the Library
steps.text: Emma goes to the library. She sees many books on the shelf. There are sixteen red books. There are seventeen blue books. How many are there? There are eighteen green books, nineteen yellow books, and twenty orange books!
steps.scene_description: young girl looking at colorful books on library shelf

questions.question: Where does Emma go?
How many red books are there?
How many orange books does Emma see?

steps.rule_pattern: How many ___ are there? / There are ___ ___.
steps.explanation: We use 'How many' to ask about numbers, and we answer with 'There are' plus the number and thing we are counting.
examples: How many books are there? There are sixteen books.
How many pencils are there? There are nineteen pencils.
How many apples are there? There are twenty apples.

items.explanation: The correct question order is 'are there'

items.sentence: There are twenty book on the shelf.

items.explanation: Use 'many' with countable things like books

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Look at this library shelf. How many books are there? Let me count them with you. There are sixteen red books on the top. There are seventeen blue books in the middle. And there are twenty green books at the bottom. Wow, that's a lot of books!

questions.question: How many red books are there?
What color are the books in the middle?
How many green books are at the bottom?

steps.prompt: Practice counting books! Say these sentences out loud.

prompts.prompt: Say: There are sixteen red books.
Say: How many blue books are there?
prompts.expected_text: How many blue books are there?

prompts.prompt: Now make your own sentence! Say: There are twenty [color] books.

questions.question_text: How many books are there? There ___ seventeen books.
Which number comes after nineteen?
Read: 'There are sixteen red books on the shelf.' How many red books are there?
True or False: We say 'How many books are there?' to ask about numbers.
Complete the sentence: How ___ pencils are there?

lessons.title: New Colors
lessons.topic: Describing the colors of a classroom rainbow
lessons.context: Describing the colors of a classroom rainbow.

steps.instruction: Let's talk about colors! Answer these questions.

questions.question_text: What colors do you see in a rainbow?
questions.hint: Think about the bright colors you see in the sky after rain!

questions.question_text: Where can you find the color red in your classroom?
questions.correct_answer: In a book or on a toy
options: In a book or on a toy
questions.hint: Look around you! Can you see something red near you?

questions.question_text: What happens when you mix two colors together?
questions.hint: Think about painting! What happens when colors touch?

items.definition: A color made from red and blue.
A color between black and white.
A color like chocolate or wood.
The color of snow.
The darkest color.
A band of many colors in the sky.
items.example: Look, a rainbow in the sky!

steps.title: The Classroom Rainbow
steps.text: Look at our classroom rainbow! How many colors are there? There are seven colors. I can see red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple, and white. There is a grey cloud and a brown bird too. My friend Emma likes the black bird best.
steps.scene_description: diverse children looking at colorful rainbow display on classroom wall with paper birds and clouds

questions.question: How many colors are in the rainbow?
What color is the cloud?
Which bird does Emma like best?

steps.rule_pattern: How many ___ are there? There are [number] [color] ___.
steps.explanation: We use 'How many are there?' to ask about numbers, and we answer with 'There are' plus the number and color.
examples: How many pencils are there? There are five red pencils.
How many chairs are there? There are ten brown chairs.
How many clouds are there? There are three white clouds.

items.correctSentence: How many white clouds are there?

items.explanation: Use 'are' with numbers more than one

items.question: How many colors ___ there in a rainbow?
There ___ two black cats.
items.explanation: Use 'are' with numbers more than one

items.question: There are eight ___ desks.
items.explanation: Color words don't change for plural

items.question: ___ chairs are there?
items.explanation: Use 'How many' to ask about numbers

steps.audio_text: Good morning, children! Today we're making a classroom rainbow. Look at all the colors! How many colors are there? There are seven colors. Can you see the purple at the top? Now let's add a grey cloud and a brown bird. Oh, and don't forget the white clouds and the black bird too!

questions.question: How many colors are in the rainbow?
What color is at the top of the rainbow?
What are the children making today?

steps.prompt: Let's practice talking about colors! Listen and repeat, then answer the questions.

prompts.prompt: Say: How many colors are there?
Answer: How many colors are in a rainbow?
Tell me: What colors do you see? Use: There are...
prompts.expected_text: There are purple, grey, brown, white, and black.

questions.question_text: Which color is this? 🟣
How many pencils are there? There ___ five purple pencils.
True or False: A rainbow has many colors.
Complete the question: How ___ chairs are there?
What color is a cloud usually? ☁️

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.topic: Counting colored objects and identifying higher numbers
lessons.context: Counting colored objects and identifying higher numbers.

steps.instruction: Let's warm up! Answer these questions about numbers and colors!

questions.question_text: How many fingers do you have on one hand?
questions.hint: Look at your hand and count each finger!

questions.question_text: Which number comes after 9?
questions.hint: Count from 1 to 10. What number comes at the end?

questions.question_text: If you have 3 red apples and 2 green apples, how many apples do you have?
questions.hint: Count the red apples first, then add the green apples!

items.definition: The number that comes after ten
The number between twelve and fourteen
The number that is five plus ten
The color you get when you mix red and blue
The color of chocolate and wood
Many colors in the sky after rain
The number that is two times ten
The color of snow and clouds
The number after eleven
The number between thirteen and fifteen
The number that comes after fifteen
The number between seventeen and nineteen
The color of elephants and clouds on rainy days
The color of the night sky
The number that comes before eighteen
The number that comes before twenty

items.question: Which color do you make when you mix red and blue?
What is the number before twenty?
How many colors can you see in a rainbow?
Which number is between eleven and thirteen?

steps.title: Emma's Colorful Crayons
steps.text: Emma has a big box of crayons. She counts them. There are twelve red crayons and fifteen blue crayons. How many crayons are there? There are twenty-seven crayons in the box!
steps.scene_description: young girl counting colorful crayons at a table, organized crayons in groups

questions.question: How many red crayons does Emma have?
What is Emma counting?
How many crayons are in the box?

steps.explanation: We use 'There are' with numbers to say how many things we can see.
examples: There are twenty brown teddy bears.

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Look at my desk. I have some colored pencils. How many are there? Let me count. There are eighteen brown pencils and fifteen purple pencils. I also have thirteen white erasers. How many pencils are there in total? There are thirty-three pencils!

questions.question: How many brown pencils are there?
What color are the erasers?
How many pencils are there in total?

steps.prompt: Practice counting colored objects! Say these sentences out loud.

prompts.prompt: Say: There are fifteen purple balloons.
Say: There are twenty grey elephants.
Look around you. How many things can you see? Say: There are ___ [color] [objects].

questions.question_text: What number comes after nineteen?
There ___ sixteen brown bears.
Which color is made by mixing red and blue?
Read: 'There are twelve white clouds.' How many clouds are there?
True or False: 'How many are there?' is the correct question to ask about counting.
